constitution.description: Create or update project constitution from interactive or provided principles input, keeping all dependent templates in sync.
constitution.argument-hint:
[quick|deep | project principles] (optional)

This command creates/updates the project constitution at .codexspec/memory/constitution.md.

Three usage modes:
1. No arguments → Interactive mode: choose exploration depth (quick/deep)
2. quick → Light exploration: config files + README + core entry points (~5-10 files)
3. deep → Full exploration: above + source code analysis + architecture patterns
4. <description> → Skip exploration, use your principles directly

Examples:
  /codexspec:constitution
  /codexspec:constitution quick
  /codexspec:constitution deep
  /codexspec:constitution Python FastAPI backend using pytest, focused on type safety

specify.description: Clarify requirements through interactive Q&A to explore and refine initial ideas
specify.argument-hint: Describe your initial idea or requirements

clarify.description: Identify unclear areas in functional specification through targeted questions before technical planning

analyze.description: Perform non-destructive consistency and quality analysis between spec.md, plan.md, and tasks.md artifacts

checklist.description: Generate custom quality checklists to validate requirements completeness, clarity, and consistency

generate-spec.description: Generate spec.md document after requirements are clarified

spec-to-plan.description: Convert functional specification to technical implementation plan

plan-to-tasks.description: Break down technical implementation plan into actionable tasks

review-spec.description: Review and validate functional specification for completeness, clarity, and quality

review-plan.description: Review and validate technical implementation plan for feasibility and consistency

review-tasks.description: Review and validate task breakdown for completeness, ordering, and TDD compliance

implement-tasks.description: Execute implementation tasks with conditional TDD workflow (TDD for code, direct implementation for docs/config)

tasks-to-issues.description: Convert existing tasks to actionable GitHub Issues for this feature

commit.description: Generate and execute Conventional Commits compliant commit messages based on git status and session context
commit.argument-hint: [-p] Use -p to only preview the message without committing

commit-staged.description: Analyze staged git changes and generate Conventional Commits compliant commit message
commit-staged.argument-hint: [-p] Use -p to only preview the message without committing

pr.description: Generate structured Pull Request (GitHub) or Merge Request (GitLab) description based on git diff and optional spec.md integration

check-i18n-semantics.description: Check semantic consistency and quality of multilingual documentation

translate-docs.description: Translate project documentation to specified language

review-python-code.description: Review Python code for PEP 8 compliance, type safety, engineering robustness, and constitution alignment
review-python-code.argument-hint:
Path to Python file or directory to review

Examples:
- `src/codexspec/` - Review entire package
- `src/codexspec/__init__.py` - Review single file
- `src/ tests/` - Review multiple paths

review-react-code.description: Review React/TypeScript code for component architecture, hooks compliance, state management, performance, and constitution alignment
review-react-code.argument-hint:
Path to React/TypeScript file or directory to review

Examples:
- `src/components/` - Review entire components directory
- `src/components/Button.tsx` - Review single file
- `src/hooks/ src/components/` - Review multiple paths

quick.description: One-stop quick implementation for small requirements — auto spec, plan, tasks, and code
